title: chaenorhinum glareosum
intro: Chaenorhinum glareosum, also known as the dusty miller or small toadflax, is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Chaenorhinum glareosum are believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as providing antioxidant and antiseptic effects. Herbal preparations of Chaenorhinum glareosum may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to soothe coughs, calm digestive issues, and promote relaxation.
